calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-04-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-23-2018 Continued To May-23-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PETRA BUCHANAN's Motion To Transfer Venue is GRANTED. The case is transferred to Mendocino County and plaintiff is to pay the requisite transfer fees. "Venue is determined based on the complaint on file at the time the motion to change venue is made." (Brown v. Sup. Crt. (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 482.) Plaintiff alleges causes of action for breach of contract as well as tort claims such as breach of fiduciary duty, conversion, and fraud. Different venue provisions apply to the tort claims and breach of contract claims. Plaintiff brings a "mixed action." "In cases with mixed causes of action, a motion for change of venue must be granted on the entire complaint if the defendant is entitled to change of venue on any one cause of action." (Id. at 488; see also Johnson v. Superior Court (1965) 232 Cal.App.2d 212.). No special venue law applies to plaintiffs' tort claims. Therefore, the opening portion of CCP 395(a) controls and those claims are properly tried at defendant's residence in Mendocino. (See Stokes v. Newsom (1948) 89 Cal.App.2d 147, 149-150.) Plaintiff's arguments regarding CCP 392 are inapposite. The complaint seeks damages and plaintiff is not asserting a real property claim despite the recordation of the lis pendens. Each side's claim for fees is denied.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
